<!-- BEGIN cc-habits (auto-generated; edit habits via `cc-habits`) -->
# Coding preferences

> Generated by cc-habits from observed coding patterns. These rules are inferences,
> not declarations, review before sharing. Best-effort redaction applied; rule text
> may reflect patterns from proprietary code.

Follow these preferences when generating or editing code in this repository.

## Code Organization

- Group related functionality into separate functions.
- Separate concerns into distinct functions for better maintainability.

## Code Style

- Omit unnecessary parameters in function calls.
- Omit unnecessary punctuation.
- Prefer a consistent naming convention for functions and variables.
- Prefer explicit TypeScript type annotations for function signatures.
- Use whitespace to separate logical sections of code.
- Prefer using function expressions with the function keyword for clarity.

## Error Handling

- Error handling should be implemented to handle potential errors.

## File Management

- Log files should be rotated to prevent disk space issues.

## Formatting

- Use a consistent formatting style for code and data blocks.

## HTML

- Include favicon and touch icons in HTML head tags.
- Include meta property og:image for social media sharing.

## Imports

- Only import necessary modules and functions.

## Lists

- Use pipes to separate items in tables.
- Use numbered lists to organize and structure items.

## Markdown

- Use headers to organize and structure markdown documents.
- Use tables to organize and structure data in markdown documents.

## Naming

- Prefer a consistent naming convention for files and directories.

## Punctuation

- Prefer using a comma instead of an em dash for list items.

## Signal Management

- Signals should be capped at 50 per session to avoid provider errors.

## Testing

- Setup and teardown steps should be explicitly defined in tests.

## Versioning

- Version numbers should be incremented.
<!-- END cc-habits -->
